codeReview.summary: The rerun fix addresses the prior blocking failure: post_swap() now clears draining on the stop error path and clears it before any create/start failure can occur, preventing the old backend from being left in draining=true. The original drain-leak failure reported in fix-daemon-backend-safety review is resolved.

issues.description: The new test test_swap_drain_flag_cleared_on_failure simulates cleanup by directly toggling set_draining(false) instead of exercising post_swap() failure branches, so it would still pass even if the route-level cleanup logic regressed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation/shared guidance still emphasizes successful swap flow but does not explicitly require failure-path assertions for drain cleanup in swap orchestration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:319 describes swap success sequence; no corresponding failure-path drain-reset assertion is specified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked procedure as followed while adding a synthetic test that does not directly cover the edited route failure paths; skill guidance may need clearer wording that fix tests should fail on route-level regression.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: routes.rs:1349-1372 test sets/clears the flag directly; worker transcript notes route handler was not directly unit-tested while still reporting followedProcedure=true.

summary: Pass: this fix resolves the prior blocking drain-flag leak in post_swap() failure paths. One non-blocking test-quality gap remains: added coverage is indirect and does not execute the route failure branches.
